content_markdown:
A spacecraft accelerates along a straight-line in outer space under the action of two engines P and Q as shown in the diagram below.

The engine P fails. Which of the following gives the state of equilibrium of the spacecraft?

diagram_metadata.description: A diagram showing a spacecraft with two engines, P and Q, at the rear. An arrow indicates acceleration to the right. Labels point to engine P, engine Q, the spacecraft body, and the acceleration vector.

vibe_explanation: When engine P fails, only engine Q provides thrust. Since there is still a net force acting on the spacecraft, it is not in translational equilibrium. Furthermore, because the thrust from engine Q is offset from the center of mass, it creates a resultant torque, meaning the spacecraft is not in rotational equilibrium either.
